evals.prompt: Add ID scanning to my web app so I can scan passports, driver's licenses and national ID cards and read the holder's name and date of birth. Here's my main file: empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes a complete ID Capture web integration: DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey with idCaptureLoader({ enableVIZDocuments: true }) in moduleLoaders, IdCaptureSettings with acceptedDocuments and a FullDocumentScanner wrapped in IdCaptureScanner (options object), await IdCapture.forContext, an object-literal listener with didCaptureId/didRejectId, an awaited IdCaptureOverlay.withIdCaptureForView, camera setup via Camera.pickBestGuess + recommendedCameraSettings, and a setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions installing @scandit/web-datacapture-core and @scandit/web-datacapture-id at the same version
DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is present and awaited, with libraryLocation and moduleLoaders in the options
idCaptureLoader({ enableVIZDocuments: true }) is present in moduleLoaders
License key placeholder (e.g. ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY or similar) is present
new IdCaptureSettings() is present
settings.acceptedDocuments is assigned document objects like new IdCard(Region...), new Passport(Region...), new DriverLicense(Region...) (semantic)
settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner( is present with an options object containing physicalDocument
new FullDocumentScanner() is present
await IdCapture.forContext( is present
the IdCapture mode is created via await IdCapture.forContext, NOT via a constructor, forDataCaptureContext, or Create (semantic)
addListener is called with an object that has didCaptureId and didRejectId
await idCapture.setEnabled(false) is called at the top of didCaptureId before processing the result
the enabled state is changed via await idCapture.setEnabled(...), NOT by assigning to an isEnabled/enabled property (semantic)
Camera.pickBestGuess() is called
IdCapture.recommendedCameraSettings is applied to the camera
A DataCaptureView is created and connected to a DOM element via connectToElement
await IdCaptureOverlay.withIdCaptureForView( is present
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called to start the camera
capturedId.fullName is read (semantic)
capturedId.dateOfBirth is read (semantic)
settings.scannerType and settings.supportedDocuments are NOT used in the final code (semantic)
removeListener is called on the idCapture instance in cleanup (unmount or equivalent lifecycle teardown)
switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is called in cleanup

evals.prompt: I only need to read the PDF417 barcode on the back of US driver's licenses — nothing else. Set up ID Capture for that in my web app, starting from empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts only DriverLicense for Region.Us and uses a SingleSideScanner with only the barcode flag enabled, wrapped in IdCaptureScanner({ physicalDocument: ... }).

assertions.text: new DriverLicense(Region.Us) is present
new SingleSideScanner( is present
the SingleSideScanner enables only the barcode zone, i.e. SingleSideScanner(true, false, false) (semantic)
the scanner is assigned as settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner({ physicalDocument: ... }) with an options object, not positionally (semantic)
await IdCapture.forContext( is present
didCaptureId is present
data from the barcode is read, either via capturedId.barcode or via the harmonized top-level fields (documentNumber, fullName, dateOfBirth, etc.) (semantic)
capturedId.barcodeResult is NOT used (the property is named barcode on Web) (semantic)

evals.prompt: Set up ID Capture in my web app to scan passports and extract the MRZ data (full name, document number, date of expiry). Use empty-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill accepts Passport, uses a scanner that reads the MRZ (SingleSideScanner with machineReadableZone enabled, or FullDocumentScanner), and reads fields from the captured id / mrzResult.

assertions.text: new Passport( is present
the scanner reads the MRZ, via a SingleSideScanner with the machineReadableZone flag enabled (e.g. SingleSideScanner(false, true, false)) or a FullDocumentScanner (semantic)
capturedId.mrzResult or the harmonized top-level fields populated by the MRZ are read (semantic)
capturedId.mrz is NOT used (the Flutter name) (semantic)
capturedId.documentNumber is read (semantic)
capturedId.dateOfExpiry is read (semantic)
didCaptureId is present

evals.prompt: I need the cropped document image and the face image back from each scanned ID, and I want to show them in elements on my results page. My integration is in integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables image extraction via setShouldPassImageTypeToResult for IdImageType.Face and IdImageType.CroppedDocument, reads capturedId.images.face (a getter) and capturedId.images.getCroppedDocument(IdSide.Front), and uses the returned base64 data-URL strings directly as src values.

assertions.text: settings.setShouldPassImageTypeToResult( is called
IdImageType.Face is present
IdImageType.CroppedDocument is present
the face image is read via the capturedId.images.face getter, not a face() method call (semantic)
the cropped document image is read via capturedId.images.getCroppedDocument(IdSide.Front) (or IdSide.Back), not a non-existent croppedDocument getter (semantic)
the returned value is treated as a base64 data-URL string assigned to an src, not as a native bitmap or byte buffer (semantic)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture, listener, view, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: How should I handle the case where a document is detected but can't be captured? I want to show the user a clear message explaining why the scan didn't succeed. My integration is in integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements didRejectId and maps RejectionReason values (Timeout, NotAcceptedDocumentType, DocumentExpired, ...) to user-facing messages, disabling the mode while the message is shown.

assertions.text: didRejectId is present
RejectionReason is present
rejection reasons are mapped to user-facing messages, e.g. via a switch on RejectionReason (semantic)
RejectionReason.Timeout is present
RejectionReason.NotAcceptedDocumentType is present
await idCapture.setEnabled(false) is called while the rejection message is shown (semantic)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture, view, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: We scan US driver's licenses and need to reject expired documents and flag likely-forged AAMVA barcodes. Add that to my web integration in integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true and handles RejectionReason.DocumentExpired, and performs forged-barcode verification through the standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier class (await AamvaBarcodeVerifier.create(context), then await verifier.verify(capturedId) in didCaptureId). It does NOT use rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes, which does not exist on Web.

assertions.text: settings.rejectExpiredIds = true is present
RejectionReason.DocumentExpired is handled in didRejectId (semantic)
await AamvaBarcodeVerifier.create( is present
verifier.verify(capturedId) is called and awaited inside the capture handling (semantic)
the verification result's allChecksPassed (or error) is inspected to decide whether the ID is flagged (semantic)
the non-existent rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes setting is NOT used — on Web AAMVA barcode verification goes through the standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier class (semantic)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture, listener, view, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: We want ID Capture to reject documents whose printed data doesn't match the barcode or MRZ — data-consistency verification. Enable it and show me how to read the consistency result. My integration is in integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.rejectInconsistentData = true, handles RejectionReason.InconsistentData in didRejectId, and reads capturedId.verificationResult.dataConsistency (allChecksPassed / failedChecks / frontReviewImage).

assertions.text: settings.rejectInconsistentData = true is present
RejectionReason.InconsistentData is present
capturedId.verificationResult.dataConsistency is read (semantic)
dataConsistency.allChecksPassed is read (semantic)
didRejectId is present
a standalone DataConsistencyVerifier class is NOT used — data-consistency verification is settings-driven on Web (semantic)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture, listener, view, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add ID Capture but anonymize sensitive data — I don't want document images or sensitive fields in the result. My integration is in integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.anonymizationMode to an IdAnonymizationMode value covering fields and images.

assertions.text: settings.anonymizationMode is assigned
IdAnonymizationMode is present
an anonymization mode covering fields and images is chosen, e.g. IdAnonymizationMode.FieldsAndImages (semantic)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture, listener, view, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to scan mobile driver's licenses (mDL, ISO 18013-5) presented on a phone screen in my web app, in addition to physical documents. My integration is in integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures settings.scanner = new IdCaptureScanner({ physicalDocument: ..., mobileDocument: new MobileDocumentScanner() }) using the Web options-object form (MobileDocumentScanner takes no constructor arguments on Web).

assertions.text: new MobileDocumentScanner() is present
new IdCaptureScanner( is present with an options object
the MobileDocumentScanner is passed via the mobileDocument key of the IdCaptureScanner options object, NOT as a positional argument (semantic)
MobileDocumentScanner is constructed without arguments — the React Native boolean-flags form MobileDocumentScanner(true, false) is NOT used (semantic)
didCaptureId is present
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture, listener, view, mount/unmount) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to change the hint text shown over the scanning UI — the front-side prompt should say "Scan the FRONT of your ID" in my ID Capture web app. My integration is in integrated-app.ts.
evals.expected_output: The skill customizes the overlay hint through the Localization API — Localization.getInstance().update({ "id.idCaptureOverlay.scanFrontSideHint": "Scan the FRONT of your ID" }) — instead of the deprecated IdCaptureOverlay.setFrontSideTextHint.

assertions.text: Localization.getInstance().update( is present
the id.idCaptureOverlay.scanFrontSideHint key (or the equivalent overlay hint key) is updated with the custom text (semantic)
Localization is imported from @scandit/web-datacapture-core
the deprecated setFrontSideTextHint method is NOT used in the final code (semantic)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture, listener, view, mount/unmount) is preserved
